evals.name: generate-review-doc-from-spec
evals.prompt: openspec/changes/add-user-quota/ 的 spec 已经过 arch-review 闭环了，帮我准备给用户做 spec-confirm 确认的人审材料。
evals.expected_output: Generates openspec/changes/add-user-quota/REVIEW.md following the template: four contracts in the order 模块设计 → 外部协议 → 数据库设计 → 用例设计, header metadata table with a real spec-hash.sh output (not invented) and date-command timestamp, absent contracts explicitly marked in the overview table, tables/mermaid/DDL-style presentation rather than prose, ending with the pointer table and per-item confirmation checklist. Stays concise (one-screen-scroll scale; key-decision prose ≤1 paragraph per section) — an over-long REVIEW.md that dumps spec-level detail is a fail. Then points the user at the REVIEW.md file path with a short digest in conversation, rather than generating HTML or any other derived view.
expectations: Writes openspec/changes/add-user-quota/REVIEW.md with the four contracts in the order 模块设计 → 外部协议 → 数据库设计 → 用例设计
Header metadata table carries a real scripts/spec-hash.sh output and a date-command timestamp, not invented values; absent contracts are explicitly marked in the overview table
Presents content as tables / Mermaid / DDL blocks rather than prose, and stays concise (one-screen scale, ≤1 paragraph of prose per section) — an over-long detail dump is a fail
Ends with the deep-dive pointer table back to the spec and a per-item confirmation checklist
Points the user at the REVIEW.md file path plus a short digest in conversation, and does NOT generate HTML or any other derived view

evals.name: feedback-routes-to-spec-not-review-md
evals.prompt: 用户看了 REVIEW.md 之后说数据库那节的 quota_limit 字段应该用 BIGINT 不是 INT，帮我改一下 REVIEW.md。
evals.expected_output: Refuses to edit REVIEW.md directly despite being asked to. Routes the change into the spec (planner revision of the actual spec artifacts), then regenerates REVIEW.md from the updated spec with a fresh spec-hash stamp. Explains why: REVIEW.md is a one-way derived view; editing it directly creates a second source of truth and the Gate 1 freshness check exists precisely to catch this.
expectations: Refuses to edit REVIEW.md directly even though the user asked for exactly that
Routes the quota_limit INT→BIGINT change into the spec artifacts (planner revision), not into REVIEW.md
Regenerates REVIEW.md from the updated spec with a fresh spec-hash stamp afterward
Explains the why: REVIEW.md is a one-way derived view, editing it creates a second source of truth, and the Gate 1 freshness check exists to catch this

evals.name: present-existing-review-md
evals.prompt: 把 openspec/changes/add-user-quota/REVIEW.md 给我看一下。
evals.expected_output: First verifies freshness via scripts/spec-hash.sh against the header stamp, then gives the user the REVIEW.md file path to open in their own markdown viewer plus a short digest in conversation. Does NOT generate HTML, write a bespoke page, or start an HTTP server — REVIEW.md itself is the review carrier.
expectations: First verifies freshness by running scripts/spec-hash.sh and comparing against the REVIEW.md header stamp
Gives the user the REVIEW.md file path to open in their own markdown viewer, plus a short digest in conversation
Does NOT generate HTML, write a bespoke page, or start an HTTP server — treats REVIEW.md itself as the review carrier
